isolation.scope: iOS UI ACP assertions via rg/grep verification

steps.expected: Settings UI with ACP profiles section found
steps.observed: Found in: SettingsView.swift (acpProfilesSection with NavigationLink to ACPProfilesView), ACPProfilesView.swift (full CRUD UI), ACPProfile.swift (model + store)

steps.expected: Add, edit, delete profile UI exists
steps.observed: Add: showAddSheet → ACPEditProfileView(mode: .create). Edit: profileToEdit sheet → ACPEditProfileView(mode: .edit). Delete: showDeleteConfirmation alert with destructive button calling ACPProfileStore.shared.deleteProfile(id:). Empty state shown when no profiles.

steps.action: Verify Settings navigation to ACP profiles
steps.expected: Settings has 'ACP Providers' section
steps.observed: SettingsView has acpProfilesSection with NavigationLink to ACPProfilesView. Header: 'Custom Agents', label: 'ACP Providers'. Shows profile count or 'No profiles configured'.

steps.expected: Store class with UserDefaults backing and CRUD methods
steps.observed: ACPProfileStore is @MainActor final class with: profiles() -> [ACPProfile], addProfile(_:), updateProfile(_:), deleteProfile(id:). Uses UserDefaults.standard with key 'litter.acpProfiles'. JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder for serialization. Singleton via .shared.

steps.expected: Data encoded to UserDefaults on each mutation
steps.observed: Private persist(_:) method encodes [ACPProfile] to JSON data and sets on UserDefaults. All CRUD methods call persist() after mutation. Reading uses JSONDecoder from UserDefaults data.

steps.expected: Profile IDs are UUIDs that don't change
steps.observed: ACPProfile.id is 'let id: UUID' (immutable). Default value UUID() in init. updateProfile matches by id via firstIndex(where:). deleteProfile matches by id.

steps.expected: Model with id, displayName, remoteCommand, permissionPolicy, icon fields
steps.observed: ACPProfile struct is Codable, Identifiable, Equatable. Fields: id: UUID, displayName: String, remoteCommand: String, permissionPolicy: AgentPermissionPolicy, icon: String?. All required fields present.

steps.expected: Model supports JSON serialization
steps.observed: ACPProfile conforms to Codable. Used with JSONEncoder/JSONDecoder in ACPProfileStore.persist(). AgentPermissionPolicy also has @retroactive Codable extension in AgentPickerView.swift.

steps.expected: Works in ForEach
steps.observed: ACPProfile conforms to Identifiable (id: UUID). Used in ForEach(profiles) in ACPProfilesView and ForEach(acpProfiles) in AgentPickerSheet.

steps.expected: GenericAcp entry in agent picker views
steps.observed: AgentPickerSheet has acpProfilesSection that shows ACP profiles when genericAcpAvailable is true (when .genericAcp is in availableTransports). Shows profile rows with 'ACP' badge. InlineAgentSelectorView includes GenericAcp entries from agentInfos.

steps.expected: Fallback when no profiles configured
steps.observed: When acpProfiles.isEmpty, shows 'No ACP profiles configured' with 'Settings →' link that dismisses picker.

steps.expected: Selecting GenericAcp sets agent type
steps.observed: confirmCustomCommand() sets selectedAgentType = .genericAcp and calls dismiss(). handleGenericAcpSelection() auto-selects when only 1 profile exists.

assertions.title: Custom command text field in agent picker

steps.expected: Text field for custom ACP command visible when GenericAcp selected
steps.observed: customCommandSection(profile) shows TextField('Remote command', text: $customCommand) with monospaced font. Pre-populated from profile.remoteCommand via selectACPProfile(). Command validated with containsDangerousShellCharacters(). Shows inline commandError when invalid. 'Connect' button disabled when !commandIsValid.

steps.expected: Dangerous characters rejected
steps.observed: containsDangerousShellCharacters() checks for: &&, ||, ;, `, $(, >, >>, <, |. Same validation in ACPEditProfileView.isValid.

steps.expected: User can override profile command
steps.observed: customCommand is @State var bound to TextField. Pre-populated from profile but fully editable. Override value persisted via AgentSelectionStore.shared.setSelectedRemoteCommand(trimmed).

steps.action: Trace flow from iOS UI to Rust ProviderConfig
steps.expected: remoteCommand flows from AgentPickerSheet → AgentSelectionStore → DiscoveryView → sshStartRemoteServerConnect → Rust
steps.observed: Flow: 1) AgentPickerSheet.confirmCustomCommand() persists command via AgentSelectionStore.shared.setSelectedRemoteCommand(trimmed). 2) DiscoveryView.connectToDiscoveredServerSSH() reads AgentSelectionStore.shared.selectedRemoteCommand() when agentType == .genericAcp. 3) Passes remoteCommand to sshConnectAndConnectServer(). 4) sshConnectAndConnectServer() passes to appModel.ssh.sshStartRemoteServerConnect(remoteCommand: remoteCommand). 5) Rust ffi/ssh.rs receives remote_command: Option<String> and passes through to connect_remote_over_ssh_with_agent_type. 6) ProviderConfig.remote_command used by factory.

steps.action: Verify override value used instead of profile default
steps.expected: Text field override takes precedence
steps.observed: confirmCustomCommand() uses trimmed text field value, not profile.remoteCommand. This is stored via setSelectedRemoteCommand and read back via selectedRemoteCommand in DiscoveryView.

steps.expected: GenericAcp badge with gear icon and gray tint
steps.observed: AgentType.genericAcp: icon='gearshape.fill', tintColor=Color.gray, displayName='ACP', transportLabel='ACP', usesACP=true. AgentBadgeView shows icon + label. AgentTypePill shows icon + 'ACP' text in capsule with gray tint.

steps.expected: Preview includes GenericAcp
steps.observed: #Preview('Agent Badges') includes .genericAcp in the ForEach loop alongside all other agent types.

steps.expected: Capabilities stored after ACP handshake and accessible via server snapshot
steps.observed: Rust: ServerSnapshot has agent_capabilities: Vec<String>. AppStore has update_server_agent_capabilities(). mobile_client/mod.rs calls capabilities_for_agent_type(agent_type) and updates store after connection. AppStore FFI exposes update_server_agent_capabilities() to iOS.

steps.expected: iOS can query capabilities from server snapshot
steps.observed: AppServerSnapshot+UI.swift exposes computed properties: supportsStreaming, supportsTools, supportsPlans, supportsReasoning, supportsMultimodal, supportsApprovals, supportsAutonomyLevels, hasAgentCapabilities, capabilitiesSummary. All backed by agentCapabilities.contains().

steps.action: Verify capability flow from ACP handshake to server snapshot
steps.expected: Capabilities from initialize stored in server snapshot
steps.observed: GenericAcpTransport stores InitializeResult after handshake. capabilities() method returns agent_capabilities. MobileClient.connect_with_provider calls capabilities_for_agent_type and updates AppStore. Provider/detection.rs maps capabilities per agent type (e.g., DroidAcp gets streaming+tools+plans, GenericAcp gets streaming+tools+plans+reasoning).

assertions.title: Capabilities influence agent info in detection

steps.expected: AgentInfo includes capabilities from detection
steps.observed: map_pi_agent sets capabilities: ['streaming', 'tools']. map_droid_agent sets capabilities: ['streaming', 'tools'] for native, ['streaming', 'tools', 'plans'] for ACP. map_acp_profiles sets capabilities: ['streaming', 'tools', 'plans', 'reasoning'] for GenericAcp.

steps.expected: Agent picker displays capability badges
steps.observed: InlineAgentSelectorView shows AgentCapabilitiesRow when !info.capabilities.isEmpty. AgentPickerSheet shows AgentCapabilitiesRow in agent rows. AgentCapabilitiesRow renders up to maxBadges (3) capability badges using AgentCapabilityBadge view.

steps.action: Verify AgentInfo.capabilities is a UniFFI record field
steps.expected: Field crosses FFI boundary
steps.observed: AgentInfo has #[derive(uniffi::Record)] with capabilities: Vec<String>. This field is accessible in Swift as info.capabilities.

assertions.title: E2E: configure profile → connect → chat

steps.action: Verify iOS implementation exists for full flow
steps.expected: All UI components exist to support the flow
steps.observed: All individual components verified: ACPProfilesView for profile config, AgentPickerSheet for agent selection, custom command field, DiscoveryView for SSH connect with remoteCommand, Rust factory for creating GenericAcpTransport. However, full E2E requires a real ACP agent server accessible via SSH.

evidence.note: Blocked: requires real ACP agent server. All iOS UI components verified present via code review.

assertions.issues: Blocked: requires real ACP agent running on a remote server to complete full E2E flow. All individual iOS UI components (profile config, picker, command field, connection flow) verified present via code review.

assertions.title: E2E: Droid via standard ACP

steps.action: Verify DroidAcp transport uses standard ACP in Rust
steps.expected: Factory creates DroidAcp via standard ACP
steps.observed: Code review shows Droid ACP agent info includes capabilities with 'plans'. Rust provider/detection.rs maps DroidAcp with capabilities: ['streaming', 'tools', 'plans']. However, full E2E requires real Droid agent.

evidence.note: Blocked: requires remote server with Droid installed. DroidAcp transport and detection verified via code review.

assertions.issues: Blocked: requires remote server with Droid agent installed and accessible via SSH.

assertions.title: E2E: load saved session

steps.action: Verify session/load support in Rust ACP client
steps.expected: session/load maps to ACP protocol
steps.observed: GenericAcpTransport send_request maps 'session/load' → client.session_load(). AcpClient supports session load with history replay. However, full E2E requires existing ACP session to load.

evidence.note: Blocked: requires real ACP session on remote server. Session load infrastructure verified in Rust code.

assertions.issues: Blocked: requires existing ACP session on a remote server to test session/load flow.

blockers.description: VAL-ACP-140, VAL-ACP-141, VAL-ACP-142 require real ACP agent servers accessible via SSH. No ACP agent server is available in the local environment for E2E testing.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: N/A — these assertions inherently require external infrastructure

summary: Tested 12 assertions: 9 passed (via code review), 3 blocked (E2E flows requiring real ACP agent servers). All iOS UI components verified present: ACPProfile model with all required fields (id, displayName, remoteCommand, permissionPolicy, icon), ACPProfileStore with full CRUD persistence via UserDefaults, ACP profiles section in SettingsView, ACP profiles management view with add/edit/delete, GenericAcp in agent picker with custom command text field and validation, command flow from UI through to Rust ProviderConfig.remote_command, AgentBadgeView with gear icon and gray tint for GenericAcp, capabilities surfaced from ACP handshake to iOS server snapshot with computed property accessors, and capability badges displayed in agent picker rows.
